title: runtime notify STATUS dashboard generation
purpose: I5.1 defines runtime notify status dashboard generation for the agent-driven expert system. Runtime & integration — IDE, SDK, headless, MCP tools.
scope: Owns `I5.1` only; siblings under `I5` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).
Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §11 — Branch I — Runtime & integration plane](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#11-branch-i-runtime-integration-plane).
behavior_steps: After journal-keeper dual-write at session end or phase completion, the conductor runs generate-dashboard ([B1.1](B1.1-s0-deterministic-mandatory-first.md) script or skill) to regenerate `STATUS.md` and `docs/operator/dashboard.md` from `state.json` and the staleness manifest.
Dashboard rows surface goal_id, `next_action`, blockers, and evidence status so operators can resume pursuit without opening the full journal.
Dashboard output is observational only per [A6.3](A6.3-operator-observe-without-unblocking-loop.md)—regeneration does not advance `next_action`, clear H2, or substitute for H3 sign-off.
IDE, SDK, and headless runtimes share the same S0 regeneration path so operator snapshots stay consistent regardless of where pursuit runs.
If `state.json` or staleness inputs are unreadable, regeneration fails closed and the conductor records the fault rather than publishing stale or empty dashboard rows.
edge_cases: Operator closes laptop mid-loop â€” state.json must resume from last good dual-write.
Concurrent manual edit to queue JSON â€” conductor reloads queue each wake; last writer wins with journal note.
Edge case `I5.1` variant 3: verify state dual-write before continuing pursuit.
Edge case `I5.1` variant 4: verify state dual-write before continuing pursuit.
Pass 3: add regression test or evidence path specific to `I5.1`.
Pass 3: cross-link related nodes in same branch index.
failure_modes: **Silent stop:** Agent ends turn without updating queue â†’ mitigated by /loop + check-hierarchy-queue.py EMPTY gate.
**False complete:** Item marked done without artifact â†’ audit-hierarchy-depth.py re-enqueues deepen pass.
**Scope bleed:** Worker edits journal/state during planning-only expansion â†’ forbidden in vision-expansion-prompt.
**Stale design:** Upstream vision Â§ changes â†’ reconcile-stale adds deepen items for affected ids.
implementation: Map `I5.1` to v2.14â€“v2.23 release row in SEC-15-index.md.
Create or extend S0 script if behavior is file-derived.
Add unit test under tests/unit/test_i5_1.py when script exists.
Validate `I5.1` against SEC-15 release checklist and parent index links.
Document `I5.1` in parent index with verify command and release tag.
Add checklist row in SEC-15 release doc for `I5.1`.
acceptance: [ ] Named script, skill, or test path exists or is listed in SEC-15 release row
narrative: I5.1 defines runtime notify status dashboard generation for the agent-driven expert system. Runtime & integration — IDE, SDK, headless, MCP tools. In practice, the system after journal-keeper dual-write at session end or phase completion, the conductor runs generate-dashboard ([b1.1](b1.1-s0-deterministic-mandatory-first.md) script or skill) to regenerate `status.md` and `docs/operator/dashboard.md` from `state.json` and the staleness manifest, then dashboard rows surface goal_id, `next_action`, blockers, and evidence status so operators can resume pursuit without opening the full journal.
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent plane-i 2026-06-28 -->

STATUS.md and `docs/operator/dashboard.md` regenerate from state.json and staleness manifest via generate-dashboard skill—S0 where script exists. Dashboards inform operators; they do not unblock pursuit ([A6.3](A6.3-operator-observe-without-unblocking-loop.md)).

Session end and phase completion trigger regeneration in journal-keeper. Dashboard rows link to goal id, next_action, blockers, and evidence status for at-a-glance resume without opening full journal.
